drugs.notes: Administer in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes using a low protein binding 0.2 micron filter. Dose banding not applicable — flat dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatitis AST/ALT 3-5x ULN and/or total bilirubin 1.5-3x ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold treatment and administer corticosteroids. Resume when LFTs at NCI-CTC grade 1 following corticosteroid taper.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatitis AST/ALT ≥5x ULN or bilirubin >3x ULN, or AST/ALT ≥50% increase lasting ≥1 week in patients with liver metastases with grade 2 elevation at baseline
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue pembrolizumab.
Withhold and administer corticosteroids. Resume when nephritis at grade 1 or below following corticosteroid taper.
Permanently discontinue.
Withhold and administer corticosteroids. Resume when colitis at grade 1 following taper.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above colitis failing to improve to grade 1 within 12 weeks
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue.
Withhold. Treat with corticosteroids 1-2mg/kg/day prednisone. Resume if event improves to grade 0-1 within 12 weeks and steroids reduced to ≤10mg prednisolone/day.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or 4 pneumonitis, or second episode of pneumonitis
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue.
Withhold and consider dermatology referral. Treat with systemic corticosteroids prednisolone 1mg/kg/day. Resume when grade 1 or below.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 4 adverse reaction or any grade 3-4 infusion related reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue.

dose_modifications.condition: Treatment interruption of 12 weeks or longer
dose_modifications.notes: Current funding approval precludes further treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer.

pre_medications: No routine antiemetics required
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV when required for infusion related reactions
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for infusion related reactions
Paracetamol 1000mg oral when required for infusion related reactions (max 4g/24h, minimum 4h between doses)
special_warnings: Dose reductions are NOT permitted for pembrolizumab — treatment is withheld or permanently discontinued.
PD-L1 tumour proportion score (TPS) ≥50% required; confirmed by approved validated test.
No prior anti-PD-1, anti-PD-L2, anti-CD137 or anti-CTLA-4 therapy (unless via pembrolizumab EAMS programme).
No active brain or leptomeningeal metastases.
Maximum treatment duration 2 years (35 cycles at Q3W).
Atypical immune responses observed — continue in clinically stable patients with initial evidence of progression until confirmed.

Systemic corticosteroids should be avoided before starting treatment due to potential interference with pharmacodynamic activity. Corticosteroids may be used after starting to treat immune-related adverse reactions.
FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle. Thyroid function tests prior to starting and before each cycle.
Immune-related adverse reactions can involve GI, liver, skin, nervous, endocrine or other organ systems — onset may be months after last dose.
Pembrolizumab — extravasation classification: neutral. Use low protein binding filter.

drugs.notes: Administer in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes using a low protein binding 0.2 micron filter.

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold treatment and administer corticosteroids. Resume when LFTs at NCI-CTC grade 1 following corticosteroid taper.

dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue pembrolizumab.
Withhold and administer corticosteroids. Resume when nephritis at grade 1 or below.

dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue.

dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue.

dose_modifications.condition: Treatment interruption of 12 weeks or longer
dose_modifications.notes: Current funding approval precludes further treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer.

pre_medications: No routine antiemetics required
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV when required for infusion related reactions
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for infusion related reactions
Paracetamol 1000mg oral when required for infusion related reactions (max 4g/24h, minimum 4h between doses)
special_warnings: Dose reductions are NOT permitted for pembrolizumab — treatment is withheld or permanently discontinued.
PD-L1 tumour proportion score (TPS) ≥50% required; confirmed by approved validated test.
No prior anti-PD-1, anti-PD-L2, anti-CD137 or anti-CTLA-4 therapy (unless via pembrolizumab EAMS programme).
No active brain or leptomeningeal metastases.
Maximum treatment duration 2 years (18 cycles at Q6W / 42-day cycle).
Atypical immune responses observed — continue in clinically stable patients with initial evidence of progression until confirmed.
Systemic corticosteroids should be avoided before starting treatment. Corticosteroids may be used after starting to treat immune-related adverse reactions.
FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle. Thyroid function tests prior to starting and before each cycle.
Immune-related adverse reactions can involve GI, liver, skin, nervous, endocrine or other organ systems — onset may be months after last dose.
Pembrolizumab — extravasation classification: neutral. Use low protein binding filter.

drugs.notes: Administer in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes using a low protein binding 0.2 micron filter. Dose banded as per agreed bands.

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold treatment and administer corticosteroids. Resume when LFTs at NCI-CTC grade 1 following corticosteroid taper.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatitis AST/ALT ≥5x ULN or bilirubin >3x ULN, or AST/ALT ≥50% increase lasting ≥1 week in patients with liver metastases with grade 2 elevation at baseline
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue pembrolizumab.
Withhold and administer corticosteroids. Resume when nephritis at grade 1 or below.
Permanently discontinue.
Withhold and administer corticosteroids. Resume when colitis at grade 1 following taper.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above colitis failing to improve to grade 1 within 12 weeks
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue.
Withhold. Treat with systemic corticosteroids 1-2mg/kg/day. Resume if event improves to grade 0-1 within 12 weeks and steroids ≤10mg prednisolone/day.

dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue.
Withhold; consider dermatology referral. Treat with systemic corticosteroids prednisolone 1mg/kg/day. Resume when grade 1 or below.

dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue.

dose_modifications.condition: Treatment interruption of 12 weeks or longer
dose_modifications.notes: Current funding approval precludes further treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer.

pre_medications: No routine antiemetics required
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV when required for infusion related reactions
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for infusion related reactions
Paracetamol 1000mg oral when required for infusion related reactions
special_warnings: Dose reductions are NOT permitted for pembrolizumab — treatment is withheld or permanently discontinued.
Indication: previously treated locally advanced or metastatic PD-L1-positive NSCLC; at least one prior chemotherapy (and targeted treatment if EGFR/ALK-positive tumour).
Stopped at 2 years of uninterrupted treatment if no documented disease progression.
WHO performance status 0 or 1.
FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle. Thyroid function tests prior to starting and before each cycle.
Immune-related adverse reactions can involve GI, liver, skin, nervous, endocrine or other organ systems — onset may be months after last dose.
Systemic corticosteroids should be avoided before starting treatment. Corticosteroids may be used after starting to treat immune-related adverse reactions.
Pembrolizumab — extravasation classification: neutral. Use low protein binding filter.

drugs.notes: Administer in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Dose banded as per CSCCN agreed bands. Pemetrexed is an inflammitant — extravasation precautions required. Drain pleural or peritoneal effusions prior to administration where possible.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <1.5x10⁹/L at start of subsequent cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover, continue at initial dose. If counts still low, reduce to 80% of original dose.
Stop treatment if myelosuppression recurs despite dose reduction.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets <100x10⁹/L at start of subsequent cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover, continue at initial dose. If counts still low, reduce to 80% of original dose.
Reduce to 50% of original dose. If platelet count remains <50x10⁹/L despite dose reductions, consider stopping therapy.
Do not administer if CrCl <45ml/min.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: total bilirubin >1.5x ULN or transaminases >3x ULN (>5x ULN if liver metastases)
dose_modifications.notes: Do not administer. Pemetrexed requires bilirubin ≤1.5x ULN and ALT/AST/AP ≤3x ULN (or ≤5x ULN with liver tumour involvement).

pre_medications: Folic acid 5mg once daily oral — start 1-2 weeks prior to first pemetrexed dose and continue for 3 weeks after the last dose (continuous throughout treatment)
Hydroxocobalamin (vitamin B12) 1mg intramuscular injection every 3 months — start 1-2 weeks prior to first pemetrexed dose (prescribed separately, not in ARIA)

Dexamethasone 4mg twice daily oral — start the day before chemotherapy (day -1), continue on day 1 and day 2 (3 days total). If forgotten pre-treatment, administer dexamethasone 20mg IV 15-30 minutes before chemotherapy.
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral (take-home)
special_warnings: POST-INDUCTION MAINTENANCE ONLY — for non-squamous NSCLC that has not progressed immediately following platinum-based chemotherapy with gemcitabine, paclitaxel or docetaxel.
Patients who received pemetrexed+cisplatin as first-line cannot receive pemetrexed maintenance.
Mandatory folic acid supplementation (5mg daily oral) and vitamin B12 (hydroxocobalamin 1mg IM every 3 months) must be in place before first dose and continued throughout treatment.
CrCl must be ≥45ml/min — do not administer if below this threshold.
Consideration should be given to draining pleural or peritoneal effusions prior to administration.
Palliative intent. WHO performance status 0 or 1.
FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle. Chest x-ray prior to each cycle.
Baseline cross-sectional imaging required prior to starting treatment.
Prophylactic antibiotics may be considered if required.
NPSA oral chemotherapy alert does not apply — IV administration only.

drugs.notes: 960mg (8 x 120mg tablets) once daily continuously. Swallow whole with water at the same time each day, with or without food. If dose missed and <6 hours since scheduled time, take as normal. If >6 hours, omit and continue next day. If vomiting occurs, do not take additional dose. Available as 120mg tablets. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert applies.

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 480mg once daily.
Reduce to 240mg once daily.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: AST/ALT grade 3 or above AND bilirubin ≥2x ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue sotorasib.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: AST/ALT grade 2 with symptoms or grade 3 or greater (without bilirubin ≥2x ULN)
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt treatment until recovered to grade 1 or below, then resume at next dose reduction level.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above diarrhoea despite supportive care
dose_modifications.notes: Stop treatment until diarrhoea resolves to grade 1 or below/baseline, then resume at next dose reduction level.
Stop treatment. Resume when recovered to ≤grade 1. Resume at next dose reduction level. If grade 4 hypertension recurs, permanently discontinue.
Permanently discontinue if recurrent grade 4 hypertension.

dose_modifications.condition: Proteinuria grade 3-4 or urinary protein ≥3.5g/24 hours
dose_modifications.notes: Stop treatment until recovered to grade 1 or baseline.
Immediately withhold and contact clinical lead. Permanently discontinue if no other potential causes identified.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above nausea or vomiting despite supportive care
dose_modifications.notes: Stop treatment until recovered to grade 1 or less, then resume at next dose reduction level.
Sotorasib not studied in moderate or severe renal impairment (CrCl <60ml/min). Use with caution.

pre_medications: Metoclopramide 10mg oral up to three times daily when required for nausea and vomiting (take-home, supply 28 x 10mg tablets cycle 1 only)
Loperamide 4mg after first loose stool, then 2mg after each subsequent loose stool, maximum 16mg in 24 hours (take-home, cycle 1 only)
special_warnings: KRAS G12C mutation MUST be confirmed by validated assay on tumour tissue biopsy or plasma specimen (liquid biopsy) prior to treatment.
Patient must have had prior platinum doublet chemotherapy and/or PD-1/PD-L1 immunotherapy.
No prior KRAS G12C-targeted therapy (unless via company early access scheme).
NPSA/2008/RRR001 National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy must be followed.
Sotorasib must only be prescribed under the supervision of a consultant oncologist.

Significant drug interactions: avoid strong CYP3A4 inducers. Co-administration of PPIs and H2 antagonists not recommended — if required, take sotorasib 4 hours before and 10 hours after acid-reducing agent.
ILD/pneumonitis risk increased in patients previously treated with immunotherapy or radiotherapy — monitor for new or worsening pulmonary symptoms.
FBC, U&Es, LFTs at baseline and every 2 weeks for first 6 weeks, then prior to day 1 of each cycle.
Formal medical review before start of second month and by end of second month of therapy.
ECOG performance status 0, 1 or 2.

protocol_name: SCLC – Tarlatamab (EAMS) – DLL3xCD3 Bispecific T-cell Engager

drugs.notes: CYCLE 1 DAY 1 ONLY — 1mg step-up dose. Reconstitute and dilute in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9%. Administer over 1 hour at constant flow rate using infusion pump. Monitor patient from start of infusion for 24 hours in appropriate healthcare setting. Patient must remain within 1-hour of healthcare setting for 48 hours from start of infusion with caregiver.

CYCLE 1 DAYS 8 AND 15 — 10mg dose. Day 8: monitor for 24 hours in healthcare setting; remain within 1 hour for 48 hours with caregiver. Day 15: observe for 6-8 hours post infusion. From Cycle 2 Day 1 and Day 15 (every 2 weeks): 10mg each dose. Observation post-infusion: Cycle 2 = 6-8h; Cycles 3-4 = 3-4h; Cycle 5 onwards = 2h (unless prior Grade ≥2 CRS/ICANS). Available as 1mg and 10mg lyophilised powder single-dose vials.

dose_modifications.condition: No dose reductions recommended for tarlatamab
dose_modifications.notes: No dose reductions are recommended. Treatment is withheld or permanently discontinued based on toxicity.
Withhold until neutrophil count improves to ≥1.0x10⁹/L and fever resolves. Consider G-CSF. Permanently discontinue if recovery does not occur within 3 weeks.
Permanently discontinue tarlatamab.
Withhold until platelet count improves to ≥50x10⁹/L with no evidence of bleeding. Permanently discontinue if recovery does not occur within 3 weeks.
Permanently discontinue tarlatamab.
Withhold until haemoglobin ≥8 g/dL.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: grade 3 ALT/AST (5.0-20x ULN) or grade 3 bilirubin (3.0-10x ULN)
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold tarlatamab until toxicity improves to ≤grade 1.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: grade 4 ALT/AST (>20x ULN) or grade 4 bilirubin (>10x ULN) or AST/ALT >3x ULN with total bilirubin >2x ULN in absence of alternative causes
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue tarlatamab.
No data available. Use only if benefit outweighs risks.

dose_modifications.condition: CRS grade 1 (temperature ≥38°C without hypotension or hypoxia)
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until adverse reaction resolves, then resume at next scheduled dose. See CRS management guidelines.
Withhold until adverse reaction resolves, then resume at next scheduled dose. When resuming, monitor for 24 hours in healthcare setting. See CRS management guidelines.
For recurrent grade 3 CRS, permanently discontinue tarlatamab.
Permanently discontinue tarlatamab.
Withhold until adverse reaction resolves, then resume at next scheduled dose. Grade 2: monitor for 24 hours when resuming.

dose_modifications.condition: ICANS grade 3 (ICE score 0-2, depressed consciousness, seizures or focal oedema)
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until adverse reaction resolves. If no improvement to grade ≤1 within 7 days or recurrent grade 3, permanently discontinue. ICU care may be required. Monitor for 24 hours when resuming.

dose_modifications.condition: ICANS grade 4 (unarousable, stupor/coma, life-threatening seizure, diffuse cerebral oedema)
dose_modifications.notes: Permanent discontinuation. ICU care. Treat convulsive status epilepticus per local guidelines.
Withhold until recovery to ≤grade 1 or baseline. Permanently discontinue if no resolution within 28 days, or consider for grade 4 events.

dose_modifications.condition: Infection (active, any grade during step-up phase)
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold tarlatamab in step-up phase until infection resolves.
Permanently discontinue tarlatamab.

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg IV injection — administer 60 minutes prior to tarlatamab on Cycle 1 Days 1 and 8 (mandatory). Not required for Cycle 1 Day 15 onwards unless resuming step-up dosing or prior CRS/infusion reaction.
Sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml IV infusion over 60 minutes — administer immediately after completion of tarlatamab infusion on Cycle 1 Days 1, 8 and 15 (mandatory).
Tocilizumab 8mg/kg (maximum 800mg) IV 8-hourly if required for CRS — one dose must be available on ward prior to each infusion. Maximum 3 doses. See CRS management guidelines.

Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily for 28 days (take-home) — for TLS prophylaxis (low-moderate risk patients). High-risk patients: consider rasburicase 3mg IV once prior to first dose followed by allopurinol 300mg daily starting the day after rasburicase.
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for infusion related hypersensitivity reactions.
Salbutamol 2.5mg nebule when required for tarlatamab-related bronchospasm.
special_warnings: CYTOKINE RELEASE SYNDROME (CRS) — severe and life-threatening CRS can occur. Step-up dosing regimen (1mg on Cycle 1 Day 1, then 10mg) is mandatory to mitigate CRS risk. Patients MUST be admitted as inpatients during Cycle 1 Days 1 and 8 (step-up dosing period). Monitor from start of infusion for 24 hours. Refer to hospital CRS management guidelines (Assessment and Management of CRS following CAR-T Therapy).

ICANS (Immune Effector Cell-Associated Neurotoxicity Syndrome) — severe ICANS can occur. Monitor neurological status. Refer to hospital ICANS management guidelines (Diagnosis and Management of ICANS POST CAR-T Therapy).

TUMOUR LYSIS SYNDROME (TLS) — risk increased in patients with high tumour burden or rapidly growing tumour. Assess TLS risk prior to each dose increment. Ensure TLS assessment completed before each dose.
STEP-UP DOSING IS MANDATORY — do not omit the 1mg step-up dose on Cycle 1 Day 1. If dose delayed, refer to re-start protocol (Table 6 in PDF) to determine whether step-up must be repeated.
This is an UNLICENSED drug in the UK supplied under manufacturer's Expanded Access Management System (EAMS) on a named-patient basis.
Bispecific T-cell engager (DLL3 x CD3) — mechanism of action differs from conventional chemotherapy and checkpoint inhibitors.
Hypersensitivity reactions reported, including rare severe events (rash, bronchospasm) — monitor during infusion.
No dose reductions are recommended for tarlatamab.
Administer using infusion pump at constant flow rate. Flush IV catheter with sodium chloride 0.9% over 3-5 minutes to ensure patency.
FBC, U&Es, Ca²⁺ and LFTs at baseline and prior to each dose.
SCLC — advanced, disease progression on or after platinum-based chemotherapy.
Treatment agreed at relevant MDT. Written consent required prior to treatment.

drugs.notes: 450mg (2 x 225mg film-coated tablets) once daily, days 1-28 inclusive (continuous). Take with or just after food. Swallow whole — do not crush or chew. If dose missed, can be taken as soon as remembered the same day unless next dose due within 8 hours. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert applies.

dose_modifications.notes: Consider withholding tepotinib until recovered and resume at reduced dose.
Withhold tepotinib until resolved, then resume at reduced dose.
Permanently discontinue tepotinib.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: increased ALT/AST without increased bilirubin — grade 3
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until recovery to baseline ALT/AST. If recovered within 7 days, resume at same dose; otherwise resume at reduced dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: increased ALT/AST without increased bilirubin — grade 4
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue tepotinib.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: ALT/AST >3x ULN with total bilirubin >2x ULN (absence of cholestasis or haemolysis)
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue tepotinib.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: increased total bilirubin without concurrent increased ALT/AST — grade 3
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until recovery to baseline bilirubin. If recovered within 7 days, resume at reduced dose; otherwise permanently discontinue.
Permanently discontinue tepotinib.
No dose adjustment required for CrCl 30-89ml/min.
No information available for CrCl <30ml/min. Use with extreme caution.
Withhold tepotinib if ILD suspected. Permanently discontinue if ILD confirmed.
Permanently discontinue tepotinib.
Maintain dose level. If intolerable, withhold until resolved, then resume at reduced dose.
Withhold tepotinib until resolved, then resume at reduced dose.
Permanently discontinue tepotinib.

pre_medications: No routine antiemetics or premedications required
Take tepotinib with or just after food
special_warnings: MET EXON 14 SKIPPING ALTERATION must be confirmed by histological/cytological biopsy or liquid biopsy using validated assay prior to treatment.
Patient must be EGFR wild type AND negative for ALK and ROS1 gene rearrangements.
PERIPHERAL OEDEMA monitoring is required — oedema is a frequent adverse effect. Monitor weight and fluid balance at each cycle. Dose reduce or withhold as clinically indicated.
INTERSTITIAL LUNG DISEASE (ILD)/PNEUMONITIS — withhold if suspected, permanently discontinue if confirmed.
HEPATOTOXICITY — monitor LFTs; dose modification required for elevated transaminases.
NPSA/2008/RRR001 National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy must be followed.
Tepotinib interacts with several medicines including metformin — always check for drug interactions.
Tepotinib may have a minor influence on ability to drive or use machinery — fatigue and asthenia reported.
FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one of each cycle.
ROS1, ALK, EGFR and MET exon 14 skipping alteration must be confirmed before initiating treatment.
Formal medical review before start of second month and by end of second month of therapy.
Severe hepatic impairment (Child Pugh C) not studied — use not recommended.
ECOG performance status 0 or 1.

drugs.notes: 2.3mg/m²/day orally once daily for 5 consecutive days (days 1-5). Doses rounded to nearest 0.25mg capsule (available as 0.25mg and 1mg capsules). Swallow whole, not chewed, with plenty of water. Short course — must not be continued beyond 5 days. Prescribed from secondary care only. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert must be adhered to.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <1.0x10⁹/L on day 1 of subsequent cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover, continue at initial dose. If counts remain low or episode of grade 3+ neutropenia, reduce dose to 1.9mg/m²/day.
Consider stopping treatment or further reducing to 1.5mg/m²/day. Treatment discontinued in clinical trials if dose needed to be reduced below 1.5mg/m²/day.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets <100x10⁹/L on day 1 of subsequent cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover, continue at initial dose. If counts still low or dropped below 25x10⁹/L, reduce dose to 1.9mg/m²/day.
Consider stopping treatment or further reducing to 1.5mg/m²/day.
Reduce to 1.9mg/m²/day for subsequent courses. Grade 2 diarrhoea may also require dose modification.
Reduce starting dose to 1.9mg/m²/day for 5 days. If well tolerated, dose may be increased to 2.3mg/m²/day in subsequent cycles.
Limited information available — seek specialist guidance.

pre_medications: Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times daily for 5 days (antiemetic — moderate emetogenicity)
Loperamide 4mg oral after first loose stool, then 2-4mg four times daily when required for diarrhoea (maximum 16mg per 24 hours)
special_warnings: NPSA/2008/RRR001 National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy MUST be adhered to — this is a short course (5 days only) that MUST NOT be continued beyond day 5.
Prescribed from secondary care only. Community staff must be made aware this is a short fixed-course treatment.
HAEMATOLOGICAL TOXICITY — significant myelosuppression expected. FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle. Neutrophils ≥1.5x10⁹/L and platelets ≥100x10⁹/L required before cycle 1.

TUMOUR LYSIS SYNDROME (TLS) — SCLC can be highly chemosensitive. Allopurinol should be prescribed for at-risk patients, starting the day before chemotherapy and continuing while significant chemosensitive tumour bulk remains (normally one cycle).
Indication: relapsed SCLC where re-treatment with first-line therapy is not appropriate AND CAV (cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, vincristine) is contra-indicated.
Palliative intent. WHO performance status 0, 1 or 2.
Doses are rounded to nearest 0.25mg capsule; alternate day dosing schedule may be used to facilitate correct dose.
Growth factors may be considered according to local policy.
Baseline chest x-ray and cross-sectional imaging required before starting treatment. Chest x-ray before each cycle.
Renal dose reduction required for CrCl 30-49ml/min (reduce starting dose to 1.9mg/m²/day).
Topotecan was discontinued in clinical trials if dose needed to be reduced below 1.5mg/m²/day.
